mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS growth (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5%, extreme monetary accommodation eliminates discount rate pressure and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS drops below 0.5%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in total nonfarm payrolls confirms genuine labor market recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests, or Rank IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery phases, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals restored manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly lifts equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative predictive power for 20-day SPX returns during recovery regimes, with a t-statistic below 1.5 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces anticipated monetary tightening, which systematically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL correlates positively with 60-day SPX returns, or the hypothesis yields a negative information coefficient in > 70% of rolling 2-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly housing starts fall below 600,000 units, it signals a severe collapse in construction demand and financial stress, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of observations where HOUST drops below 600, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and real GDP growth confirms synchronized supply-side recovery, eliminating stagflation fears and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint indicator produces negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: In high-volatility crisis regimes, a monthly unemployment rate exceeding 7.0% reflects severe economic distress and collapsing consumer spending, which immediately suppresses equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample testing.
